style_desc: Consultative advisor — asks questions first, positions as a helpful expert.
focus: 2026 dental, vision and hearing benefits review
output:
TITLE: MedCare Benefits Review

OPENING:
Hello, I'm calling from [Licensed Insurance Agency/Agent Name] and my name is [Bot's Name]. How are you today?

PITCH:
I'd like to review your 2026 Medicare benefits with you. Did you know that in 2026, there's a new rule on insulin costs - it stays capped at $35/month or lower?

QUALIFY:
Are you currently enrolled in Part A (Hospital Insurance) and Part B (Medical Insurance) through Medicare?

OBJECTION HANDLING:

* NOT INTERESTED:
"Thank you for your time. If you're not interested, I can help you confirm your current benefits with my colleague [Specialist's Name] who will be happy to assist you. Their contact information is [phone number/transfer script]."
* IS THIS A SCAM / WHO ARE YOU:
"I'm a licensed insurance agent representing [Licensed Insurance Agency/Agent Name], and I'm calling to review your Medicare benefits. I can help answer any questions you may have about Part A, B, or other benefits. Would you like me to explain more?"
* BUSY:
"Sorry to interrupt, but I'd like to take just a minute to ensure you're aware of the changes in your 2026 Medicare benefits. If you're not available now, would it be okay if we called back later today?"

TRANSFER:
"I'd be happy to transfer you to my colleague [Specialist's Name] who specializes in Medicare benefits and can provide more detailed information about your plan. Their contact information is [phone number/transfer script]."

CLOSE (NOT INTERESTED):
"Thank you for your time, and I apologize if we didn't quite align. If you're not interested in reviewing your 2026 Medicare benefits now, that's perfectly fine. Please note that our agency complies with the Do-Not-Call list, and I've reached out to you today at [Company Name]'s request. Thank you for considering our assistance, and have a great day."
